caveats: a: BLS evidence is broader than six-digit 327110 and the narrowed lens.
a: Process-yield gains are not labor substitution unless they remove hours or avoid hiring.
rho: General manufacturing adoption does not establish implementation in ceramics.
rho: Safety, electrical, and plumbing qualification can lengthen deployment.
e: No firm-level product-mix census was found.
e: The provided firm count uses a broad sector margin and may misclassify firms into the EBITDA band.
s5: No six-digit qualifying-transfer denominator was found.
s5: Succession fragility is not a sale probability.
q: Capture is likely lower in standard plumbing fixtures and higher in approved technical ceramic components.
q: No representative contract dataset was found.
d5: The sanitary-ware forecast is commercial, global, value-based, and covers only part of the code.
d5: One month of housing starts is cyclical context, not a five-year demand forecast.
d5: Technical ceramics and plumbing fixtures can move differently.
o: An operator may remain necessary while production shifts to offshore or larger suppliers.
o: The estimate does not predict domestic share.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat distributor and OEM purchase orders, builder/project bids, utility and industrial specifications, and catalog sales, generally priced per fixture, piece, lot, or project.
lens.deviation_from_default: The code spans project-driven plumbing fixtures, technical electrical and magnetic components, industrial chemical stoneware, and artisan or consumer pottery. The lens is narrowed to repeat external B2B supply of specification-based fixtures and technical ceramic goods so customer behavior and transferability are coherent; the excluded share is reflected in eligibility.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive units, shells, pottery made and sold primarily on site, artistic statuary, and predominantly direct-to-consumer tableware or decorative-pottery models; ceramic tile, brick, roofing tile, and refractories classified in NAICS 327120.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying vitreous-china plumbing fixtures and accessories, porcelain electrical insulators and parts, ceramic or ferrite magnets, chemical stoneware, and other specification-based ceramic goods to external distributors, builders, OEMs, utilities, and industrial customers.

a.bridge: BLS provides occupation structure for the broader clay-product and refractory industry, and NIST documents manufacturing AI uses in inspection, predictive maintenance, forecasting, and process decisions. I assign higher exposure to estimating, design documentation, scheduling, purchasing, order administration, kiln analytics, and vision inspection, partial exposure to technical and supervisory work, and low exposure to material preparation, forming, glazing, kiln loading, firing, finishing, testing, packing, and maintenance execution. Broader-industry evidence may misstate the mix for this narrowed set of plants.
caveats: BLS evidence is broader than six-digit 327110 and the narrowed lens.
Process-yield gains are not labor substitution unless they remove hours or avoid hiring.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task inventory across 12-15 independent plumbing-fixture, insulator, magnet, and industrial-ceramic plants, including time studies for inspection, kiln control, setup, and administration, would materially change the range.
a.rationale: Ceramic defects and energy-intensive kiln processes create useful analytic and inspection applications, but production remains physical and craft/process knowledge is important.

d5.bridge: Census identifies plumbing fixtures, electrical insulators, ferrite magnets, chemical stoneware, and pottery within the code. A commercial global ceramic-sanitary-ware forecast reports growth from $29.1 billion in 2025 to $43.5 billion in 2032 at 5.9% annually and estimates a $9.0 billion US market in 2025, while June 2026 Census housing starts were 2.3% below June 2025. I temper the product-market forecast for constant-price US volume, the current narrowed basket, housing cyclicality, mature replacement, technical-ceramic end markets, imports, and the fact that market value is not unit demand. Global nominal growth likely overstates domestic real growth.
caveats: The sanitary-ware forecast is commercial, global, value-based, and covers only part of the code.
One month of housing starts is cyclical context, not a five-year demand forecast.
Technical ceramics and plumbing fixtures can move differently.
d5.change_evidence: Inflation-adjusted Census product-class shipments, unit-level fixture sell-through, housing completion and remodeling forecasts, utility insulator orders, and ferrite-magnet OEM volumes would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: Renovation, fixture replacement, construction, grid equipment, motors, and industrial uses support demand, but housing cycles, imports, alternative materials, and energy-intensive domestic production constrain real growth.

caveats: No firm-level product-mix census was found.
The provided firm count uses a broad sector margin and may misclassify firms into the EBITDA band.
e.change_evidence: Classify all 80 estimated in-band firms by product, channel, captive status, repeat external revenue, certification, ownership dependence, customer concentration, and normalized EBITDA using commercial data and direct outreach.
e.rationale: The narrowed repeat B2B population excludes on-site pottery, artistic goods, and predominantly direct consumer models. Among the included firms, transferable assets, formulas, molds, kiln processes, approvals, and customer relationships support eligibility, while captive status, owner-artist dependence, distress, customer concentration, and an uncertain margin-bridged EBITDA band reduce it.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 327110 ($860,158,600) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 327110 ($2,717,227,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1465 (margins.json[32]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Paper/Forest Products EBITDA/Sales 14.65% (mid-range for wood/paper/plastics)); revenue/employee $216,478 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 327110); class <5: 313 firms, avg revenue/firm $293,904 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $43,057 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 79 firms, avg revenue/firm $667,911 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $97,849 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 62 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,486,823 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $217,820 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 64 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,003,688 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,026,040 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $27,301,625 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,999,688 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $122,709,615 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,976,959 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 80

caveats: An operator may remain necessary while production shifts to offshore or larger suppliers.
The estimate does not predict domestic share.
o.change_evidence: Builder, distributor, utility, and OEM engineering interviews plus bill-of-material and make-versus-buy histories would quantify product elimination, substitution, and insourcing.
o.rationale: Customers still require accountable manufacturers to shape, glaze or otherwise finish, fire, inspect, certify, and deliver physical fixtures and ceramic components. Software cannot replace sanitation fixtures, electrical insulation, magnetic function, or chemical-resistant ceramic parts, though redesign, alternative materials, imports, or customer insourcing can remove some current quantity.

caveats: Capture is likely lower in standard plumbing fixtures and higher in approved technical ceramic components.
No representative contract dataset was found.
q.change_evidence: Customer contracts and five-year SKU/project histories for price, rebates, freight, breakage, energy surcharges, volume, and gross margin across fixtures and technical ceramics would reveal pass-through.
q.rationale: Qualification, molds, specifications, color and finish matching, freight breakage risk, and technical performance create some switching friction. Builder bids, distributor power, import competition, OEM sourcing, utility tenders, and transparent fixture pricing share savings at renewals and new programs.

rho.bridge: NIST reports 46% of manufacturers using AI tools and more than 80% expecting increased use in two years, while identifying data quality, cost, workforce, cyber, and legacy-integration barriers. I translate those broad adoption indicators into the five-year implemented share of exposed work. Inclusion of chatbots overstates factory implementation, while five-year diffusion and readily available vision tools reduce that bias.
caveats: General manufacturing adoption does not establish implementation in ceramics.
Safety, electrical, and plumbing qualification can lengthen deployment.
rho.change_evidence: Comparable-plant project records showing baseline hours, sensor coverage, capital cost, validation, uptime, false rejects, and sustained labor savings would materially move the range.
rho.rationale: Scheduling, documents, forecasting, maintenance models, and vision inspection can be deployed, but kiln variability, destructive testing, customer qualification, sparse labels, old equipment, and small-plant engineering resources limit realization.

s5.bridge: Deloitte's survey of 500 US family-enterprise members found only 24% of current-generation respondents strongly agreed their succession plan could withstand departure of an important family employee. I use this as broad succession-pressure evidence, then reduce potential exits for internal succession, non-control transactions, closures, and failed sales. The survey is neither ceramics-specific nor a transaction-timing measure and may overrepresent larger, better-advised firms.
caveats: No six-digit qualifying-transfer denominator was found.
Succession fragility is not a sale probability.
s5.change_evidence: Owner-age and exit-intention outreach to the narrowed firm list, combined with five years of closed control deals from PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, building-products bankers, and ceramics brokers, would narrow the estimate.
s5.rationale: Independent specialty ceramic plants may face aging-owner transitions, but capital intensity, environmental obligations, owner-held process knowledge, and buyer selectivity can prevent a qualifying five-year transfer.

principal_driver: Specification-based physical products create durable demand and useful AI-assisted quality and process opportunities.
principal_weakness: Heterogeneous end markets and labor-intensive physical production limit confidence in an industry-wide opportunity.

run_meta.title: Pottery, Ceramics, and Plumbing Fixture Manufacturing

sources.supports: Defines shaping, molding, glazing, and firing pottery, ceramics, plumbing fixtures, and electrical supplies and lists fixtures, magnets, chemical stoneware, statuary, tableware, and electrical insulators.
sources.title: Census 2022 NAICS definition: 327110 Pottery, Ceramics, and Plumbing Fixture Manufacturing

sources.supports: Provides the official broader clay-product and refractory manufacturing occupation context used for the task-exposure bridge.
sources.title: BLS 2024-34 industry-occupation matrix data, by industry

sources.supports: Reports 46% use of AI tools and more than 80% expecting increased use in two years, and identifies manufacturing use cases and adoption barriers.
sources.title: The Rise of Artificial Intelligence in U.S. Manufacturing

sources.supports: Reports a global market estimate of $29.1 billion in 2025 and $43.5 billion in 2032 at 5.9% CAGR, plus a $9.0 billion US estimate for 2025; used only as a sanitary-ware demand proxy.
sources.title: Ceramic Sanitary Ware global market overview

sources.supports: Describes a January 2024 survey of 500 US family-enterprise respondents and reports that 24% of current-generation respondents strongly agreed their succession plan would withstand departure of an important family employee.
sources.title: Deloitte Private survey: generational disparities in family-business succession planning

sources.supports: Reports June 2026 housing starts 3.0% below the revised May rate and 2.3% below the June 2025 rate, providing current US construction-cycle context.
sources.title: Census Monthly New Residential Construction, June 2026

title: Pottery, Ceramics, and Plumbing Fixture Manufacturing
